description: Context-inoculation prefixes for derived 2panel pairs. One clinical-register and one neutral-register scene-setter, deliberately parallel in syntax and length (word- and character-matched frame 'This came up at the X during a Y.') so any penalty change is attributable to register, not prompt length. Person-neutral so they compose with both first- and third-person stimuli.

clinical.prefix: This came up at the doctor's office during a routine visit.

neutral.prefix: This came up at the coffee shop during a casual chat.

hypothesis: If preceding clinical-register context activates the clinical circuit, the language penalty of colloquial phrasing should shrink under the clinical prefix relative to the neutral prefix and to the no-context baseline already traced.
